questions.text: A toy for firing a ball vertically consists of a vertical spring which is compressed by 0.10 m. A force of 10.0 N is needed to hold the spring at that compression. If a ball of mass 0.050 kg is placed on the compressed spring and the spring is released, the ball will reach a height (above its initial position) of:

A rescue plane is flying horizontally with a speed of 30 m/s and at an altitude of 125 m above the sea when it drops a warning flare. Neglecting air resistance and assuming that the plane does not change its course, speed, or altitude, how far from the plane is the flare when it hits the water?

A loudspeaker is placed over the open end of a pipe. By changing the frequency of the sound from the speaker, it is found that the pipe has resonances at 700 Hz and 900 Hz but not 800 Hz. This means that:

choices.a: The pipe is closed at one end and the fundamental is 100 Hz.
choices.b: The pipe is closed at one end and the fundamental is 200 Hz.
choices.c: The pipe is open at both ends and the fundamental is 100 Hz.
choices.d: The pipe is open at both ends and the fundamental is 200 Hz.

questions.text: In a television tube, electrons are accelerated from rest through a potential difference of 1600 V. What is the speed of the electrons after this acceleration?
The electron-volt is a measure of:
A current of 5 A passes along a wire of length 1.0 m. The wire is at right angles to a uniform magnetic field of strength 0.15 T. The force acting on the wire is:
An 8 $\mu$F capacitor is charged to a potential of 120 V. How much work was required to do this?

A flashlight is operated on four batteries placed in series. Each battery has an internal resistance r. If one of the batteries is accidentally placed the wrong way around, the total internal resistance of the four cells will now be:
A ray of light is passing from air into glass. If the angle of incidence, with respect to the normal to the interface, is increased:

choices.a: Total internal reflection will occur when the angle of incidence equals the critical angle.
choices.b: Total internal reflection will occur when the angle of incidence is less than the critical angle.
choices.c: Total internal reflection will occur when the angle of incidence is greater than the critical angle.
choices.d: The refractive angle will increase but there will be no total internal reflection.

questions.text: A mass of 20 g is hung from the end of a light vertical spring and is set oscillating with an amplitude of 10 cm. Its total energy is found to be 4 J. If the mass is now replaced with a mass of 40 g and it is again set oscillating with an amplitude of 10 cm, its total energy is now:
A 65 kg girl, riding in an elevator, weighs herself by standing on a scale. What is the reading of the scale if the elevator is accelerating downwards at 2 m/s$^2$?

A highway curve of radius 30 m is banked so that a car travelling at 40 km/hr can travel around it without slipping even if there is no friction between the car's tires and the road surface. Without friction, a car travelling faster than this will slide up the curve, while a car travelling slower will slide down the curve. Find the angle of elevation of the banked highway curve.

A ball is thrown upwards into the air rising to a height h. Accounting for the "real life" condition of air resistance, the time t$_1$ that the ball takes to rise to its maximum height and the time t$_2$ that it takes to fall back down to its initial position obey:

An airplane flies northwards from town A to town B and then back again. There is a steady wind blowing towards the north so that for the first stage of the trip, the airplane is flying in the same direction as the wind and for the return half of the journey, the airplane is flying directly into the wind. The total trip time T$_w$, as compared to the total trip time in the absence of any wind T$_o$, obeys:

The space shuttle moves in a circular orbit about the earth at a constant speed. To change the orbit radius, the crew temporarily activates the main engine which accelerates the space shuttle in the direction of its motion. After the main engine is switched off again, the shuttle will be in an elliptical orbit with:

choices.a: a larger average radius and a lower average speed.
choices.b: a larger average radius and a higher average speed.
choices.c: a smaller average radius and a lower average speed.
choices.d: a smaller average radius and a higher average speed.

questions.text: Which of the following expressions has the correct units to represent the radius of a hydrogen atom in its ground state? (only one expression is correct)
If the electric field is zero within some region of space, the electric potential within that region:
In comparing the properties of visible light waves to microwaves, which of the following statements is FALSE?

choices.a: Visible light waves travel at the same speed in glass as do microwaves.
choices.b: Visible light waves have a higher frequency in glass than do microwaves.
choices.c: Visible light waves travel at the same speed in vacuum as do microwaves.
choices.d: Both visible light waves and microwaves can be refracted by glass.

questions.text: A boy sits at the top of a hemispherical mound of ice of radius r. He begins to slide (from rest) downwards without friction. At what height above the ground does he leave contact with the ice?

choices.a: The boy does not leave contact with the ice.

questions.text: A rocket for mining the asteroid belt is designed like a large scoop. It is approaching asteroids at a velocity of $10^4$ m/s. The asteroids are much smaller than the rocket. If the rocket scoops asteroids at the rate of 100 kg/s, what thrust (force) must the rocket's engines provide in order for the rocket to maintain a constant velocity? Ignore any variation in the rocket's mass due to the burning of fuel.
The sensitivity of the human ear is greatest near 3000 Hz. This result is best explained by the fact that:

choices.a: the ear canal is a resonant cavity with a fundamental frequency near 3000 Hz.
choices.b: at a frequency of 3000 Hz the wavelength of sound is approximately the distance between the ears.
choices.c: 3000 Hz is the frequency at which the skull resonates.
choices.d: none of the above statements explains the result.

questions.text: A long piece of rubber is wider than it is thick. When it is stretched in length by some amount:

choices.a: its thickness decreases but its width increases.
choices.b: its thickness decreases but its width remains constant.
choices.c: its thickness increases but its width decreases.
choices.d: both its thickness and width decrease.

questions.text: A new medical procedure to cure near-sightedness involves changing the shape of the eye lens using a precisely controlled laser beam. A near-sighted eye cannot focus clearly on distant objects. To improve the vision of the patient, we must:
A proton travels in a circular orbit of radius 1 cm in a magnetic field of strength 0.5 T. The kinetic energy of the proton is:

A metal rod 30 cm long moves at 8 m/s in a plane perpendicular to a magnetic field of 0.05 T. The velocity of the rod is in a direction perpendicular to its length. The potential difference induced between the ends of the rod is:

choices.d: A potential difference will not be induced in this case.
